title: The One With The Fake Party

lines.text: Monica and Rachel's erm, Chandler and Joey's, Chandler, Monica, and Phoebe are there. Phoebe is stressing out about something.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is it hon?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I-I can't find anything that I want to eat! Everything I eat makes me nauseous! I'm telling you, being pregnant is no piece of cake-ooh! Cake! (Chandler shrugs, and Phoebe grimaces.) No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aww, honey I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: God! Ooh! What is that smell? It's coming from the bathroom. Ooh! (She goes to the bathroom.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow! Pregnancy does give you some weird cravings.
[Cut to the bathroom, Joey is taking a shower and Phoebe knocks on the door.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's me. It's Phoebe. Listen there's something in here I want to eat, what-what smells so good?
Joey sticks his head out of the shower curtain.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is it the shampoo? It's guava.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (she smells his head) No!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! Wait-wait! (Reaches inside the shower.) Is it my bologna sandwich?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes. Yes. Yes. I can't believe it! The baby wants bologna! Maybe he wants me to eat meat? I can't eat meat!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, wait-wait! (Reaches into the shower again.) Maybe it's a pickle?!
Phoebe grimaces at the smell.
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Chandler, Monica, Joey and Rachel are there. Rachel is writing something on a pad, and then crumples it up and throws it on the floor.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you writing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, Joshua's coming in tomorrow and since I don't have the guts to ask him out, I'm going to sell him a coat and put this note in the pocket.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah? (He grabs the pad and starts reading it.) Joshua, give me a call sometime, guys like you (Pause) never go out of style-what did you throw away?
Ross and Emily enter.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi guys!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, what have you guys been up too?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, we went to see a collection of Victorian doorknobs and the Cupert-Hewitt museum.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Without me?!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: My uncle dragged us there. But, it actually it turned out to be really interesting.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: They were so ornate and beautiful, I mean look at that! (Shows them a doorknob she has.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know how museums work in England but, here, you're not supposed to take stuff.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I uh, I got it from the gift shop. They have really lax security there. (Chandler is shocked.) It's a joke. (They all laugh.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Bye. (They kiss.)

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Right, I've got to be off, I'll see ya. Buh-bye then. (She leaves.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! You guys seem to be having a good time.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah, she's-she's amazing. And-and she's so much fun. And! Y'know what? When I'm with her, I'm fun! I even signed up for helicopter classes. (Chandler is shocked.) She's leaving in two days, I don't have to do it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no, two days, you must be bummed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, she's got to go back to London. But you know what? I've been prepared for this from the start. We both knew we had two weeks together, and that's it. Y'know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey that's what all my relationships are like.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but in Ross's case, they both know in two weeks that's it.
Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pheebs!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (starts rubbing her belly) Hello! Hello!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (angered by the rubbing) Yes! I know! I know! Yeah! So the baby is totally craving meat. This afternoon I tried tricking it, I made it a soy-burger to make it think it was getting meat, y'know? And I got nauseous.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Maybe that's because soy-burgers suck!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Being pregnant is tough on your tummy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, but at least you got that cool, pregnant lady glow.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's sweat. You throw up all morning, you'll have that glow too.
Bloomingdale's, Rachel is preparing to slip Joshua the note.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: (coming in from a changing room) Okay!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, here's that trench-coat that you wanted.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Oh great! (He tries on the coat.) Wow! Yeah, it's comfortable.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Man, I could really flash somebody in this thing. (He goes to put his hands in his pockets.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (stopping him) Oh no-no, no-no, they don't want you to put your hands in the pockets until you are out of the store.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Why not?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, that's because of a lot of...(She imitates someone picking their nose and placing the treasure found in the pockets.)

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Y'know, they ruin it for everybody.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Y'know, I wore that cashmere sweater on a date last night.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Yeah, it was my first date since the uh, since the divorce.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, congratulations, so do you love her?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: No, no, no, she's nice but, y'know, it just it made me realize that I'm just not, I'm just not ready to be dating, y'know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh. Well, uh, that's uh, that's interesting. (She goes over and retrieves her note.)

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: (noticing her) Hey-whoa-hey-hey, what was that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it's just an anti-theft device.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Then uh, what's-what's this? (Shows her the real anti-theft device.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You need that, you need that too 'cause obviously, a thief could just tear this up. (Rips up the note.)
Monica and Rachel's erm, Chandler and Joey's, Ross is writing on the Magna-Doodle as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oops, sorry. Listen, we-we have to have a party tonight! Actually, we have to have one in five minutes, so everybody cancel your plans.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, what' going on?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We have to have a surprise Bon Voyage party for Emily. But it's actually for Joshua. (Starts handing out party hats.) Look, he said he's not ready to date, so I had to invite him to a party if I wanted to see him outside of work, and now I have the perfect opportunity to seduce him! (Hands Ross a party hat.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, as much as I'd like to meet Josh and warn him, Emily and I aren't going to be here. All right? I mean, she's going to come by first to say good-bye, and then I've got a whole special evening planned. So I'm sorry, no party.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Awwww!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: (entering) Hello?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Surprise!!!

lines.speaker: Chandler and Joey
lines.text: Surprise!!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: No one's ever thrown me a surprise party before!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, it was all Ross's idea.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: You're so sweet! And I'm so surprised!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You really didn't know?
[Cut to later, the party is in full swing. Rachel is in the kitchen and Chandler goes over to talk to her.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why are you in here if Joshua is all the way over there? (Points to the living room.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uhh, because I'm trying to play hard to get. Oh, quick he's looking over here, say something funny.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Like what?
Rachel laughs hysterically.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What-what-what is so funny?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I said, "Like what?"

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now that's a thinker.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, y'know what, y'know what? This playing hard to get thing is not working. Umm, hand-hand me those cherries. (Chandler does so.) Okay. Okay. (She does a little sexy walk over to where Joshua is standing.) Hi!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Care for a cherry?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Oh, no thanks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No? Y'know, I can tie one of these into a knot using just my tongue.
She tries to demonstrate this unique ability, but only succeeds in choking on it.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: You okay? (Rachel swallowed it whole and is not hacking like a heavy smoker in the morning.) You all right? (Rachel walks away, coughing.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (interrupting Monica and Emily) So we should probably get going soon.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh, but the party's only just getting started!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, but we-we have to be at the Four Seasons for drinks in 15 minutes and then y'know, then The Plaza for dinner.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: So why did you plan a party at the same time?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no-no, no, umm, actually American surprise parties are-are-are very short. It's usually, "Surprise!" And then, "Oh my God, I'm so surprised-good-bye!"

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: But Ross, I'm such having a great time! Your sister has just been telling me that you used to dress up like little, old ladies and hold make-believe tea parties.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica said that did she? (He squeezes Monica's knee really hardly and Monica winces in pain.)
[Cut to Joey and Phoebe in the kitchen. Phoebe is watching Joey make a sandwich.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, yeah. Then what are you going to put on top of that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A little salami.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh yeah! Then umm, what goes on top of the salami?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pastrami.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oooh, yeah. You're a genius.
Rachel enters, she has changed clothes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, could somebody give me a hand with this zipper?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. (He goes over to her.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Up!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You changed?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I did. I needed my lucky dress.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And lucky means, more cleavage?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Does for me.
Joey starts wiping lint off of her back, but goes at little too far and Rachel just glares at him. He stops, gives her the 'okay' symbol and walks away.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, God! Look at him, he's so cute. I wanna go over there, grab him, and kiss him! How can I kiss him and not letting him know that I like him?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! I know how you can get him, take off your bra.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: There was a seen in Footloose...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Flashdance.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah-yeah, yeah, with that-that uh, plumber girl...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She was a welder.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? Were you like in the movie, or... Anyway, she takes off her bra under her shirt and pulls it out the sleeve. Very sexy, and classy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Or if you want to kiss him, umm, you could use mistletoe.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's not Christmas!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Or Spin the Bottle?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He's not 11!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: (with her coat on, she's leaving with Ross) Thank you so much for this. It was really so thoughtful of you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? You're leaving?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, we have something we have to get to.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Uhh, yeah, I think I'm going to take-off too.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! You guys can't leave yet! You have to stay, we-we got the whole big thing planned!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What big thing?
[Cut to later, the whole group is seated on the floor and Rachel is explaining the rules of Spin the Bottle.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (spinning the bottle) So, Spin the Bottle works like this: I spin the bottle, it lands on Gunther, so I would have to kiss Gunther. (She crawls over to where Gunther is sitting and sees the look of anticipation on Gunther's face and decides not to kiss him.) All right. Who wants to go first?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I'll go.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yay!
Emily spins the bottle and it lands on Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Welcome to America. (They both kiss.)
Joey spins the bottle and it lands on Emily.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Two in a row! You've got to use your tongues now! (They kiss again.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yay! Emily!
Emily spins the bottle and once again, it lands on Joey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are the odds? What are the odds?
They both move to kiss again.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (stopping them) Okay, that-that's enough! Y'know, let's, let's let someone else play.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: If you didn't want to play, why did you come to the party?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, my turn!!

Rachel spins the bottle and it lands on....wait for it....Joshua. (You thought I was going to say Ross, didn't you?) Rachel squeals in delight and starts a slow sexy crawl over to Joshua, making sure he and everyone else watching gets a good look at her cleavage.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!! The baby just kicked!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Ohh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's okay! It's okay! It kicked once, it'll kick again!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh my God!
They all stand up and go over to Phoebe to feel the baby, preventing Rachel from kissing Joshua.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, well, everybody just remember where they were sitting.
She crawls over to Joshua and kisses the back of his knee. He feels it and looks down, Rachel pretends she's knocking a bug off his leg.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Just a bug.
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's erm, Chandler and Joey's, it is later in the party. The Spin the Bottle game is over and Chandler is making a sandwich as Phoebe watches.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know it doesn't matter how much I'm craving it. Y'know why I'm never gonna eat meat? Because it's murder, cold blooded murder.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.
He takes a bite out of the sandwich and as he does so, Phoebe attacks the other end and starts devouring the sandwich.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There's a Phoebe on my sandwich! (He walks away, giving the sandwich to Phoebe.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Phoebe, what-what are you doing?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't help it. I need the meat. The baby needs the meat.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right look, y'know how-y'know how when you're dating someone and you don't want to cheat on them, unless it's with someone really hot?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, totally!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right. Okay. Well this is the same kind of deal. If you're going to do something wrong, (He grabs two steaks from the freezer) do it right!
[Cut to later, Phoebe is finishing off her steak.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Feel better now?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, but at what cost? Six more months, three meals a day, I'm gonna eat like, y'know millions of cows.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, what if I said, I could even things out for ya, meatwise.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I eat a lot of meat right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, suppose until the baby's born I laid off it. No extra animals would die, you-you'd just be eating my animals.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey, I can't believe you would do that for me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Absolutely! I could be a vegetarian. There's no meat in beer, right?
[Cut to Ross and Emily standing by the foosball table.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, we could still make dinner if we skipped the appetisers and asked for our check right away.
Rachel enters, she has changed once again. This time into her high school cheerleading uniform.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: But, we can't go now. It looks like Rachel's gonna put on a skit.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! Have you lost your mind?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No-n-n-n-no! I am finally thinking clearly. My lucky dress wasn't working out to well for me, but for four years, this baby never missed.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel-Rachel-Rachel I-I cannot, I can't let-(pause), actually I kinda want to see what happens.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Nice uh, costume.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, yeah, well I wanted to give Emily a big American good-bye cheer. So okay! (Runs into the living room) Ready! Okay! Gimme an 'E!'

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: E!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Gimme an 'M!'

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: M!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Gimme an 'I!'

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: I!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Gimme an 'L!'

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: L!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Gimme a 'Y!'

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Y!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What do you get? (She throws her pom-poms to Joey and Phoebe and performs a cartwheel.) Emily!! (Tries to do another one.) Emil-Whoa!! (She falls in Chandler's room.) Okay! So that's me as a cheerleader! Ta-dum! (Gunther's the only one that claps.)
Joey and Monica rush over to her.

lines.speaker: Joey and Monica
lines.text: Are you all right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm fine! I'm fine! I'm just losing a tooth, it's no big deal. I have a dentist! Y'know. I'm gonna go put some ice on it. Excuse me. (She goes over to the ice and Joey and Monica follow her.) What do I do now? What do I do now?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I think you're done.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, time to take off the bra. (She glares at him.)

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Umm, that was really great, but I-I gotta take-off actually.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (trying not to be obvious) Take the bra off.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, come on, let's go get your coat.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Okay.
They both go to Chandler's bedroom to get his coat.
[Cut to Gunther and Emily.]

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Rachel is my girlfriend.
[Cut to Chandler's bedroom.]

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: So, this was uh, really fun.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, yeah! Real fun. (She makes a decision.) Y'know, this bra... Really, bothers me. (She starts taking off her bra.) Y'know, this used to be my bedroom. Yeah. A lot of memories in here, a lot of memories. If these walls could talk, y'know what they'd say? Wanna hear some memories? (She is now violently pulling on her bra in order to remove it, but it isn't co-operating.)

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Need uh, need a little hand there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no-no-no! No, I got this all under control.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: You really don't seem like you do. That's...
She is still yanking on the bra, but it is stuck in her sleeve. Finally, she gives up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ughhhh!! Forget it! (Sits down heavily on the bed.) This is, this is not how this is supposed to happen.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Well, what was supposed to happen?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Can you not look at me when I say this? (He turns around) I thought that if I could get you here, I could seduce you.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Huh. Oh, boy! (Sits down next to her.) Uhh, I-I don't wear suits to work, and I bought six of them from you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I'm sorry, I thought you needed them!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: No, no-no, no-no, my point is that I kept coming back because, I wanted to see you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why?!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Because I-I like you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You like me?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Yeah! I mean you're-you're beautiful and smart and sophisticated-a lot of this isn't based on tonight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah but-but-but you liked me! Oh my God, I can't believe this, all this time, I liked you and you liked me!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no-no-no don't say but! No-no, but's never good! Let's just leave it at, you like me and I like you.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Okay uh, however...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, now see that's a fancy but.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: My marriage like just ended, and I'm really not ready to get into anything yet.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But....

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: I'm sorry, I, I just need a little time.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.
The hallway, Ross is sitting on the step drinking a beer as Rachel comes out of the guys apartment.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, here you are. I was looking for you before. Joshua's gone so you and Emily are free to go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's okay. She's still in there enjoying her fake party and uh, it's too late to do any of the things I had planned, so...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Ross, I'm sorry. I completely ruined your evening.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, if it makes you feel any better, I made a fool out of myself.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Helps a little.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Is there room on that step for a pathetic loser?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, have a seat.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's okay, I mean it was just two-week thing anyway, I just didn't want it to end this way, y'know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, maybe you didn't want it to end?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You seem to really like her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I really do. Yeah, but what am I gonna do, I mean we-we both agreed that it was gonna be a two-week thing, y'know no commitment.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, that girl just spent the entire evening talking to your friends, asking to hear stories about you, looking through Monica's photo albums, I mean you don't do that if you're just in it for two weeks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You think?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, you got like 14 hours until she has to be at the airport, and you're sitting here in the hallway with a 28-year-old cheerleader with a fat lip.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, you're right.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks. (He starts to go inside and stops.) What photo album was it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know, it was you and a bunch of albino kids.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God! Those weren't albino kids, that was computer camp! Rach! (He hurries inside and Chandler is taking out the garbage.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You're a pathetic loser, right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh-ho, yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sit!
He does so, and immediately starts looking pathetic.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Hi. (He has just returned.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my gosh, Joshua!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: All those things I said about not being ready...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: They're not true?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: No, they're-they're all true.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Oh, I love that but.
They move to kiss, but realise that Chandler is staring at them. Chandler urges them on.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You wanna go inside and have some coffee?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. (Joshua goes inside and to Chandler.) Every time.
Closing Credits
Monica and Rachel's erm, Chandler and Joey's, the next day. Phoebe is busy making a sandwich.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oooh, what you got there?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Pastrami.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-ho-ho, yeah! Hey! Y'know what goes good with that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hm-mm, corn beef.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh, I was gonna say bologna, but that's much better. How about a little of that smoked turkey?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (He starts looking longingly at the sandwich.) Oh mama! Uh when-when is the baby due?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Six months.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ugh. Now if a cow should die of natural causes, I can have one of those right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Not if I get there first.
End
